articles.title: China Indicates Tariffs Were Discussed at Trump Summit
articles.summary: China’s Ministry of Commerce said Saturday that the countries had struck a preliminary agreement to reduce some tariffs, seemingly contradicting statements by President Trump

articles.title: Trump Calls Xi a ‘Friend.’ But He Left China Without Any Breakthroughs.
articles.summary: The lack of concrete agreements with Beijing shows the risks of President Trump’s personality-driven foreign policy, which rests on the belief that he can defend U.S. interests through charm and force

articles.title: Cassidy Loses Senate Primary in Louisiana, as Trump Vanquishes G.O.P. Foe
articles.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, a two-term Republican who voted to convict President Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial, could not muster enough votes to continue to a runoff next month.

articles.title: New York Magazine reviewing work of writer facing plagiarism allegations
articles.summary: The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.

articles.title: 7 Republicans Voted to Convict Trump. Most Are No Longer in Office.
articles.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy’s defeat means no more than two of them will be left in Congress next year.

articles.title: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy loses in Republican primary, does not advance to runoff
articles.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted for President Trump's impeachment after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.

articles.title: Senate Ruling Threatens Ballroom Funding in G.O.P. Budget Bill
articles.summary: Democrats announced Saturday night that the Senate’s top parliamentary referee had determined that the $1 billion provision did not comply with budget rules.

articles.title: How a Nature Cruise Turned Into a Nightmare
articles.summary: The hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius set off alarms for a world still traumatized by Covid. For those on board, the danger was much closer.

articles.title: Venezuela Extradites Billionaire Tycoon Alex Saab to U.S.
articles.summary: The extradition of Alex Saab, who is tied to a huge graft scheme, is part of a purge of powerful figures who helped the deposed president, Nicolás Maduro, stay in power.

articles.title: Napoleon Solo holds off Iron Honor to win the Preakness
articles.summary: It's the colt's first victory of the year. Kentucky Derby winner Golden Tempo's exit from the race left the field of 14 horses wide open to contest the middle jewel of the Triple Crown.

articles.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
articles.summary: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

articles.title: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision Song Contest as Israel Comes Second
articles.summary: Five countries boycotted this year’s contest over Israel’s involvement, but politics took a back seat once the show in Vienna got underway.

articles.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
articles.summary: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.

articles.title: Life on the edge: why Indonesians living near volcanoes won't leave
articles.summary: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.

articles.title: A new Ebola outbreak has already killed 87 people in Democratic Republic of Congo
articles.summary: Health officials are working to contain a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC of a virus strain with no known vaccine.

articles.title: North America poised for light display of aurora borealis
articles.summary: The splashy nighttime phenomenon will be best observed on Saturday and Sunday nights

articles.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead off Danish coast
articles.summary: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded o

articles.title: This Republican voted to convict Trump. Now he's up for reelection. Can he survive?
articles.summary: Louisiana Sen. Bill Cassidy was one of seven Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump. Now he's running for reelection in a race that will test Trump's hold on the GOP.

articles.title: The war in Ukraine is at a standstill as societal fatigue grows in Russia
articles.summary: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.

articles.title: The townspeople of Vilseck, Germany, worry that Trump may pull out 5,000 U.S. troops
articles.summary: President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.

articles.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint U.S.-Nigerian mission
articles.summary: Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.

articles.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
articles.summary: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
